deliverables: Infinity pool structural and hydraulic design
Full architectural plan set (plans, sections, elevations, details)
Fire protection and life safety drawings
Municipal building permit submission — Los Cabos
Weekly site visits and progress reports
RFI response and change order management
Final punch list and project closeout

payment_schedule.name: Concept Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: DG-07 Concept design approved by Marcela and client

payment_schedule.name: Executive Plans Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: DG-10 Executive plans approved + construction documents delivered

responsibilities_matrix.responsible_party: Oficio Taller (coordinated with licensed structural engineer)
Oficio Taller (coordinated with licensed MEP engineer)

exclusions: Furniture, fixtures, and equipment (FF&E) procurement and installation
Interior design and decorating beyond architectural finish specifications
Landscape architecture design (may be contracted separately)
Environmental remediation if stream impacts are identified beyond design setbacks
Off-site infrastructure improvements (road access, utility connections beyond property line)
Cost of hydrologic study, topographic survey, and soil study (client responsibility)

revision_assumptions.phase_1: 2 rounds included. Additional rounds: USD 5,000 per round
revision_assumptions.phase_2: 2 rounds included. Additional rounds: USD 5,000 per round
revision_assumptions.phase_3: 1 round included. Engineering changes driven by permit corrections handled via change order.
revision_assumptions.phase_4: 1 round included. Post-approval changes require change order at USD 8,000 minimum.

project_type_clauses.title: Hospitality Compliance Coordination
project_type_clauses.text: Oficio Taller will coordinate with applicable hospitality licensing authorities (Secretaría de Turismo, municipal tourism office) to ensure the architectural program meets operational licensing requirements for boutique hotel classification. Client is responsible for obtaining the operating license; Oficio Taller provides the architectural compliance documentation required for submission.

project_type_clauses.title: Brand Standards and Guest Experience
project_type_clauses.text: Where the client has specified guest room sizes, amenity program, and experiential intent, Oficio Taller will design to those standards. Any future brand affiliation requirements (franchisor standards, international flag standards) imposed after contract signing will constitute a scope change requiring a written change order.

project_type_clauses.title: Environmental Impact Authorization (MIA)
project_type_clauses.text: Commercial hotel projects in BCS coastal zones require an environmental impact authorization (Manifestación de Impacto Ambiental) from SEMARNAT. Oficio Taller will prepare the architectural input documentation for the MIA. The MIA submission, environmental consultant fees, and SEMARNAT process timeline are outside Oficio Taller's control; delays in MIA approval do not constitute a breach of this agreement.

project_type_clauses.title: Hydrologic Setback Compliance
project_type_clauses.text: The presence of a stream on the property requires compliance with federal riparian zone regulations (Zona Federal Maritimo Terrestre and/or federal stream setbacks per CNA/CONAGUA). Oficio Taller will design to the setback boundaries established by the hydrologic study. Any program elements displaced by required setbacks will be resolved via design adaptation at no additional fee for one revision cycle.

project_type_clauses.title: IP Rights — Hospitality Project
project_type_clauses.text: Architectural drawings, renderings, and design documentation produced by Oficio Taller remain the intellectual property of Oficio Taller until all milestone payments under this agreement are received in full. Upon final payment, the client receives a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use the construction documents for the construction and operation of the specific hotel project described herein. Client may not reproduce or adapt the design for additional projects without a new agreement.
